hypothesis: If LLaMA-3.1-8B, Gemma-2-9B, and OLMo-2-7B models are first supervised fine-tuned on BCC/B2 composition triples and then fine-tuned via Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical rewards with β=0.5, then the DPO-tuned models will generate BCC/B2 alloy candidates with higher mean reward, greater compositional validity, improved coverage and novelty, and a higher unique-pair generation rate than SFT-only models and baseline methods because DPO aligns model output preferences to physics-based synthesis objectives while mitigating harmful distribution shift.
